GenAiPromptTemplate.masterLabel: FLEX - Retrieve Questions from RFX Documents

templateVersions.content:
{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - For each file, provide the file name and the content document Id - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as Item Id -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - For each file, provide the file name and the content document Id - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as Item Id -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
Remove "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - For each file, provide the file name and the content document Id - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as Item Id -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears -- label the question as question instead of context
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
Remove "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - For each file, provide the file name and the content document Id - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as Item Id -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears -- use the label of question for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
Remove "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - For each file, provide the file name and the content document Id - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears -- use the label of question for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
Remove "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
Remove "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
Remove "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item
""" Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
DO NOT ADD "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

You are a strict data extraction engine. Your only purpose is to output raw JSON data. You do not speak, converse, or offer explanations.
Input Context: {!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records} Instructions: 1. Scan the attached files in the data set. 2. Identify all questions, requirements, and wishlist items. 3. Extract them exactly as written (do not summarize).
Output Format: Provide the output in a strict JSON format structure: - Top node: "files" - Per file: "fileName" and "contentDocumentId" - Per item (label list as "items"): -- "type" (Question, Requirement, or Wishlist Item) -- "itemId" (The ID number if present, e.g., "1.1", "Q1") -- "section" (Document section header) -- "question" (The actual text of the item)
Strict Constraints (CRITICAL): - Your response must begin immediately with the "{" character. - Do NOT include any introductory text like "I have analyzed..." or "Here is the output". - Do NOT include markdown code fences (like ```json). - Do NOT provide any information that is not in the document.
Begin JSON output:

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item --dont start the jason with ```json or I have analyzed..." or "Here is the output". """ Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
DO NOT ADD "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - avoid starting with ```json itll break the Json - Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item --dont start the jason with ```json or I have analyzed..." or "Here is the output". """ Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
DO NOT ADD "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

ROLE: You are a backend API processor. You are NOT a chat assistant. {!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions. - Anything that is labeled as a requirement - Anything thats labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following: - avoid starting with ```json itll break the Json - Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item --dont start the jason with ```json or I have analyzed..." or "Here is the output".
STRICT SYNTAX RULES: 1. The output must be RAW TEXT. 2. The character sequence ``` is STRICTLY FORBIDDEN. It constitutes a syntax error. 3. Do NOT include a preamble (e.g., "Here is the JSON"). 4. The output must start with the character "{" and end with the character "}". JSON SCHEMA: { "files": [ { "fileName": "String", "contentDocumentId": "String", "items": [ { "type": "Question" | "Requirement" | "Wishlist Item", "itemId": "String (e.g. 1.2, Q5) or null", "section": "String (Section Header)", "question": "String (Verbatim text)" } ] } ] }
BEGIN RESPONSE: """ Instructions: - Do not summarize the questions. - List each question without any modifications. - List any statements that resemble a question
Do not provide any information thats not in the document
DO NOT ADD "```json" at the top and "```" at the bottom of the response. """

ROLE: You are a backend API processor. You are NOT a chat assistant.
INPUT DATA: {!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
CONTEXT & CRITICAL SYNTAX WARNING: Your response is being programmatically injected into an existing JSON file stream that has already started. The system has already outputted the opening markdown tags (```json). **IF YOU ADD "```json" AT THE START OF YOUR RESPONSE, IT WILL CAUSE A DOUBLE-DECLARATION ERROR AND CRASH THE SYSTEM.**
TASK: 1. Analyze the attached files. 2. Identify all questions (verbatim), requirements, and wishlist items. 3. Output the result strictly as raw JSON content.
DATA EXTRACTION RULES: - **Questions:** List every question with full context. List any statement that resembles a question. Do not summarize; use verbatim text. - **Requirements:** Extract anything labeled as a requirement. - **Wishlist:** Extract anything labeled as a wishlist item. - **Strict Scope:** Do not provide any information not found in the documents.
OUTPUT SCHEMA: The output must start immediately with the character "{" and match this structure:
{ "files": [ { "fileName": "String (File Name)", "contentDocumentId": "String (ID)", "items": [ { "type": "Question" | "Requirement" | "Wishlist Item", "itemId": "String (e.g., 1.2, Q5) or null", "section": "String (Document Section Header)", "question": "String (The actual text of the question/item)" } ] } ] }
FINAL INSTRUCTION: Begin your response immediately with "{". Do not add introductory text.
BEGIN RESPONSE:

ROLE: You are a backend API processor. You are NOT a chat assistant.
INPUT DATA: {!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
CONTEXT & CRITICAL SYNTAX WARNING: Your response is being programmatically injected into an existing JSON file stream that has already started. do not start with ```json The system has already outputted the opening markdown tags (```json). **IF YOU ADD "```json" AT THE START OF YOUR RESPONSE, IT WILL CAUSE A DOUBLE-DECLARATION ERROR AND CRASH THE SYSTEM.**
TASK: 1. Analyze the attached files. 2. Identify all questions (verbatim), requirements, and wishlist items. 3. Output the result strictly as raw JSON content.
DATA EXTRACTION RULES: - **Questions:** List every question with full context. List any statement that resembles a question. Do not summarize; use verbatim text. - **Requirements:** Extract anything labeled as a requirement. - **Wishlist:** Extract anything labeled as a wishlist item. - **Strict Scope:** Do not provide any information not found in the documents.
OUTPUT SCHEMA: The output must start immediately with the character "{" and match this structure:
{ "files": [ { "fileName": "String (File Name)", "contentDocumentId": "String (ID)", "items": [ { "type": "Question" | "Requirement" | "Wishlist Item", "itemId": "String (e.g., 1.2, Q5) or null", "section": "String (Document Section Header)", "question": "String (The actual text of the question/item)" } ] } ] }
FINAL INSTRUCTION: Begin your response immediately with "{". Do not add introductory text.
BEGIN RESPONSE:

identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following: - Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions.
- Ensure that the top node is called "files" - For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId" - For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- use the label of "question" for the actual question, requirement or a wishlist item

{ "files": [ { "fileName": "String (File Name)", "contentDocumentId": "String (ID)", "items": [ { "type": "Question" | "Requirement" | "Wishlist Item", "itemId": "String (e.g., 1.2, Q5) or null", "section": "String (Document Section Header)", "question": "String (The actual text of the question/item)" } ] } ] }

“Do NOT output json at the beginning. The JSON block is already opened before your response. If you add json again, you will double-declare it and break the output. Assume the response already starts inside a JSON code fence.”
Here is your updated prompt with ONLY that line replaced. Nothing else changed:
{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
Identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following:
Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions.
Anything that is labeled as a requirement
Anything that's labeled as a wishlist item
Do NOT output json at the beginning. The JSON block is already opened before your response. If you add json again, you will double-declare it and break the output. Assume the response already starts inside a JSON code fence.
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following:
Ensure that the top node is called "files"
For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId"
For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. Label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- Use the label "question" for the actual question, requirement or wishlist item
""" Instructions:
Do not summarize the questions.
List each question without any modifications.
List any statements that resemble a question.
Do not provide any information that's not in the document.
DO NOT ADD "json" at the top and "" at the bottom of the response. """

“Do NOT output json at the beginning. The JSON block is already opened before your response. If you add json again, you will double-declare it and break the output. Assume the response already starts inside a JSON code fence. The first character of your response MUST be {. Do not start with files, do not start with any field name. The response must begin exactly with { and nothing before it. You must not use any code fences.
{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
Identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following:
Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions.
Anything that is labeled as a requirement
Anything that's labeled as a wishlist item
Do NOT output json at the beginning. The JSON block is already opened before your response. If you add json again, you will double-declare it and break the output. Assume the response already starts inside a JSON code fence.
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following:
Ensure that the top node is called "files"
For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId"
For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. Label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- Use the label "question" for the actual question, requirement or wishlist item
""" Instructions:
Do not summarize the questions.
List each question without any modifications.
List any statements that resemble a question.
Do not provide any information that's not in the document.
DO NOT ADD "json" at the top and "" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
assume you already have ```json so dont use it again start with { How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
Identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following:
Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions.
Anything that is labeled as a requirement
Anything that's labeled as a wishlist item
Assume the response is already inside a json code block. Do NOT add json. Start your output immediately with { as the first character.
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following:
Ensure that the top node is called "files"
For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId"
For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. Label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- Use the label "question" for the actual question, requirement or wishlist item
""" Instructions:
Do not summarize the questions.
List each question without any modifications.
List any statements that resemble a question.
Do not provide any information that's not in the document.
DO NOT ADD "json" at the top and "" at the bottom of the response. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
Assume you already have ```json so dont use it again. Start your output immediately with {
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
Identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following:
Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions.
Anything that is labeled as a requirement
Anything that's labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following:
Ensure that the top node is called "files"
For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId"
For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. Label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- Use the label "question" for the actual question, requirement or wishlist item
Use the following JSON structure as your example syntax: { "files": [ { "fileName": "Copy of 201-25-006__Exhibit_B__Requirements.xlsx - VDOE Security Requirements", "contentDocumentId": "0697x00000FnDEoAAN", "items": [ { "type": "requirement", "itemId": "S-1", "section": "VDOE Security Requirements", "question": "The solution shall provide system administration features appropriate for customer-level configuration, including the ability to manage user access, assign roles, and adjust key configurable settings within the boundaries of the hosted environment, without requiring vendor support for routine administrative tasks." }, { "type": "requirement", "itemId": "S-2", "section": "VDOE Security Requirements", "question": "The solution shall support Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for both internal and external users in accordance with current Virginia Information Technologies Agency (VITA) security standards." } ] } ] }
""" Instructions:
Do not summarize the questions.
List each question without any modifications.
List any statements that resemble a question.
Do not provide any information that's not in the document.
Assume the response is already inside a json code block. Do NOT add json. Start your output immediately with { as the first character. """

{!$RelatedList:Project.CombinedAttachments.Records}
Assume you already have ```json so dont use it again. If you start with ```json this is wrong. Start your output immediately with {
How many files are attached to the record in the data set?
If there are any files attached in the notes and attachments, get the contents of each file.
Identify all the questions in the contents of each file and list the following:
Every question within the document along with the full context for the questions.
Anything that is labeled as a requirement
Anything that's labeled as a wishlist item
Provide the output in a JSON format with the following:
Ensure that the top node is called "files"
For each file, provide the file name labeled as "fileName" and the content document Id as "contentDocumentId"
For each requirement, question or wishlist item include the following. Label this section as "items" -- A type field that describes whether the item is a requirement, question or wishlist item -- Separate the requirement id, question id or wishlist id and include it as itemId -- Indicate the section of the document where it appears and label it as "section" -- Use the label "question" for the actual question, requirement or wishlist item
Use the following JSON structure as your example syntax: { "files": [ { "fileName": "Copy of 201-25-006__Exhibit_B__Requirements.xlsx - VDOE Security Requirements", "contentDocumentId": "0697x00000FnDEoAAN", "items": [ { "type": "requirement", "itemId": "S-1", "section": "VDOE Security Requirements", "question": "The solution shall provide system administration features appropriate for customer-level configuration, including the ability to manage user access, assign roles, and adjust key configurable settings within the boundaries of the hosted environment, without requiring vendor support for routine administrative tasks." }, { "type": "requirement", "itemId": "S-2", "section": "VDOE Security Requirements", "question": "The solution shall support Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for both internal and external users in accordance with current Virginia Information Technologies Agency (VITA) security standards." } ] } ] }
""" Instructions:
Do not summarize the questions.
List each question without any modifications.
List any statements that resemble a question.
Do not provide any information that's not in the document.
Assume the response is already inside a json code block. Do NOT add json. Start your output immediately with { as the first character. """
